calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-29-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christy Styer and Donna Quan for Ann Mahon (Petitioner).

The Court stated that there was a question regarding the request for extraordinary fees, that there were a number of continuances due to the issue regarding the $200,000.00 held in joint tenancy, and that there was no concern with the tax issue or the sale of the property. Ms. Quan stated that this was an unusual matter, that the estate had been opened for almost 10 years and that there was no cooperation from previous Counsel. In addition, she stated that the bank records had been destroyed, that they had to figure out how the $200,000.00 amount came about, that they had to reconstruct the accounting and that the time records were detailed. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Capozzoli.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Jan-14-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donna Quan for and with Frank Mahon (Petitioner).

Ms. Quan stated that she had spoken with the Examiner, that there was a question about the American Century Investment Fund, that there was another date of sale in 2003, that she did provide the carry value of Gold Corp. in the November 2003 Schwab statement, that she didn't know why Schwab did not have the carry value, and that she filed an answer in writing to the Examiner's notes (copy provided in open court). In addition, she stated that Mr. Mahon was trying to obtain evidence that the CD accounts with Bank of America did belong to the decedent's estate, that they were following Probate Code sections 5302(a) and 5130 as to joint accounts, and that the statute requires clear and convincing evidence. Mr. Mahon stated for the record that the parties (husband and wife) had no children and that they kept things simple; if one was to die then everything went to the other and vice versa. The matter was taken under submission. Ms. Quan stated that she had answered all the notes and that there was no objection by any beneficiary.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda/R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy B. Mann.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1
Off Calendar Dec-22-2008 Continued To Jan-13-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donna Quan for the Petitioner.

Ms. Quan stated that the 2nd supplement to the petition had been filed. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aquino.
